title: Currency Risk Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tool for assessing foreign exchange exposure and quantifying potential financial impact using ISO 31000, Lean, and Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Exposure Amount (in base currency)
inputs.businessContext: Total value of foreign currency receivables or payables subject to exchange rate fluctuation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total value of foreign currency receivables or payables subject to exchange rate fluctuation.

inputs.label: Annualized Volatility of Exchange Rate
inputs.businessContext: Historical or implied standard deviation of daily log returns annualized (ISO 31000 risk metric).

businessContext_i18n.en: Historical or implied standard deviation of daily log returns annualized (ISO 31000 risk metric).

inputs.label: Confidence Level for VaR Calculation
inputs.businessContext: Statistical confidence level used in Value at Risk (VaR) estimation, typical values 90%, 95%, 99%.

businessContext_i18n.en: Statistical confidence level used in Value at Risk (VaR) estimation, typical values 90%, 95%, 99%.

inputs.label: Hedge Ratio
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of exposure covered by hedging instruments (forwards, options). Lean metric for waste reduction.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of exposure covered by hedging instruments (forwards, options). Lean metric for waste reduction.

inputs.label: Time Horizon (days)
inputs.businessContext: Risk assessment period aligned with business planning cycles (WERC supply chain standard).

businessContext_i18n.en: Risk assessment period aligned with business planning cycles (WERC supply chain standard).

inputs.label: Enable Lean Volatility Adjustment
inputs.businessContext: Applies a Six Sigma-based correction factor to account for process variation in hedging execution.

businessContext_i18n.en: Applies a Six Sigma-based correction factor to account for process variation in hedging execution.

rules.message: Exposure amount must be at least 1,000 USD.
Annualized volatility must be between 0.5% and 50%.
Hedge ratio must be between 0% and 100%.
Time horizon must be between 1 and 365 days.

thresholds.message: Value at Risk exceeds 5% of exposure – consider increasing hedge ratio.
Volatility is above 20% – high risk environment, review hedging strategy.
Hedge ratio below 30% – significant unhedged exposure, potential for large losses.

formulas.description: Convert annualized volatility to daily volatility using square root of time (ISO 31000).
Z-score corresponding to the selected confidence level (normal distribution).
Six Sigma based correction for process variation (if enabled).
Value at Risk (VaR) over the time horizon, adjusted for lean factor.
Expected Shortfall (CVaR) – average loss beyond VaR (tail risk).
Effectiveness of hedging based on hedge ratio (Lean metric).
Primary risk metric – weighted combination of VaR and Expected Shortfall, adjusted for confidence.

hiddenLossDrivers: Volatility clustering – potential regime shift not captured by constant volatility
Basis risk between spot and forward rates
Liquidity risk in exotic currency pairs
suggestedActions: Increase hedge ratio to at least 70% to reduce tail risk
Implement rolling hedge program to smooth execution costs
Use options collar strategy to limit downside while retaining upside
Monitor macroeconomic indicators (interest rate differentials, trade balances)
